name: Number of partnerships
explanation: After careful review of the research data, 75 distinct organizations were identified as having official AI-related partnerships with Vodafone Group plc. Each entity was validated against the definition and checked for direct or subsidiary relationships, de-duplicating subsidiaries, country-based variants, and mergers (e.g., multiple Vodafone divisions, Fastweb + Vodafone treated as one entity). Only those with clear evidence of an official partnership concerning AI initiatives were included; ambiguous or non-AI-related collaborations were excluded.

name: Formal governance structure
answer:
- Global Responsible AI Committee: sets AI policy, mitigates risks, and identifies/selects use cases; security teams collaborate under its governance.
- AI Leadership Council: oversees responsible AI use, sets data‑access policies, and ensures secure/private handling of customer data.
- High‑risk AI steering: for high‑impact AI cases, a company‑wide steering committee (including reputational, regulatory and legal experts) provides oversight. Secure AI lifecycle requirements are embedded in strategic AI platforms and internally developed AI applications.
explanation: Vodafone states that security teams collaborate under a global responsible AI committee that agrees policy, mitigates threats, and selects AI use cases, and that secure AI lifecycle practices are integrated into platforms and internal apps [1]. Additional claims describe an internal AI Leadership Council that oversees responsible AI use and sets data‑access policies [5], and note that high‑risk AI cases are escalated to a company‑wide steering committee with reputational, regulatory and legal experts, supported by a common ML framework to monitor use cases [7].
definition: Oversight mechanisms and committees for AI strategy, compliance, and lifecycle

name: AI Ethics documentation
answer: Vodafone has formal AI ethics documentation: a Group Artificial Intelligence (AI) Framework (responsible AI framework) introduced in 2019, a pan‑European ethical AI policy and controls, and an “AI Ethics by Design” approach. The documentation is governed by a global responsible AI committee and applies across multiple countries/markets, products and services, and extends to suppliers and partners. It sets lifecycle requirements (e.g., risk assessment, transparency/explainability, bias mitigation, and human oversight) and includes internal controls, a use‑case library, best‑practice templates, and audit documentation.
explanation: Claims state Vodafone’s work with AI is guided by a formal Group Artificial Intelligence (AI) Framework, among the first of its kind when introduced in 2019 [5], and that it has implemented pan‑European ethical AI policy and controls as part of an AI Ethics by Design approach spanning borders, products, services, cultures and jurisdictions, used by internal teams and suppliers/partners [1]. Governance is via a global responsible AI committee [3]. The documentation defines lifecycle requirements (risk assessment, transparency, lack of bias, human oversight) [12][3] and includes internal controls, a use‑case library, best‑practice templates, and audit documentation [6]. The Group Data Governance Committee also approved a new artificial intelligence policy to underpin the AI Framework, reinforcing formal documentation [16].
definition: Existence and scope of formal AI ethics guidelines and documentation

name: CAPEX & of Total Revenue
explanation: Used latest full-year figures: FY24  capex of €6,3bm (capital additions) and FY24 revenue of €36,717m. CAPEX % ≈ 17.2% [1]. Vodafone’s reporting treats capital expenditure separately from licences and spectrum, consistent with using capital additions for capex [3].
definition: Capital expenditures as a percentage of company revenue

name: 2 year revenue growth CAGR for latest fiscal year
explanation: 2-year CAGR: Calculated from FY2022 to FY2024 revenues, ended December 31, 2024 (or closest reported date for companies with non-calendar fiscal years)
definition: Compound annual growth rate of revenue  for the last two years

name: Free Cash Flow Margin for latest fiscal year
explanation: Free Cash Flow Margin: Represents the ratio of free cash flow to revenue for the full year 2024, using fiscal year-end data or the last set of audited annual results.
definition: Free cash flow - as a ratio to total revenue

name: Share Price
explanation: The “average share price” in your tables is defined as the arithmetic mean of the daily closing prices of each company’s publicly traded stock over the specified period—specifically, from July 2024 to June 2025.
definition: 12 month average stock price [+ the month start 2024 and month end 2025]

name: Number of publicly advertised in HR/job postings?
explanation: I systematically reviewed and counted all unique AI-related roles publicly advertised by Vodafone Group plc in HR/job postings, removing duplicate and ambiguous entries, and confirmed each posting's relevance to both AI maturity and the correct corporate entity. After filtering, 80 distinct postings meet the criteria, based on explicit evidence of AI-related responsibilities and confirmed affiliation with Vodafone Group plc or its strategic arms/subsidiaries such as VOIS, Vantage Towers, and VodafoneThree. The total excludes any postings lacking direct linkage to Vodafone or ambiguous AI relevance. All sources are official Vodafone career portals or reputable platforms such as LinkedIn, Glassdoor, and job boards explicitly referencing Vodafone Group plc.

definition: How many AI-related roles are publicly advertised in HR/job postings?

name: Number of Board members with AI experience
explanation: A detailed review of Vodafone Group plc's current Board of Directors, as described in annual reports and investor relations materials, shows that while several board members (e.g., Simon Segars, Deborah Kerr, Amparo Moraleda) have strong general technology backgrounds, none have proven AI expertise. Specifically, no board member has led an AI/ML or data-science organization, held a recognized academic post in AI, or published AI research. This is confirmed by the provided relations data and corroborated by the cited sources. Therefore, the number of board members with proven AI expertise is 0.
definition: Total count of individuals currently serving on the company’s official Board of Directors or Supervisory Board (as per annual report or investor website) with poven AI Expertise
output_variable: Number of Board members with AI experience

name: Number of patents since 2020
explanation:
All company results are based on a uniform WIPO PATENTSCOPE query identifying AI-related patents filed or granted since 1 Jan 2020.
The search was performed globally using the same parameters for all 40 companies.
Each query targeted group- or holding-level names and filtered for AI-relevant terms in titles, abstracts, claims, and descriptions.

Generic query used: ALLNAMES:("<<Company Name(s)>>") AND AD:[01.01.2020 TO "*"] AND EN_DE:("AI" OR "ML" OR "DL" OR "artificial intelligence" OR "machine learning" OR "deep learning" OR "neural network" OR "transformers" OR "reinforcement learning" OR "predictive model" OR "federated learning" OR "natural language processing" OR "NLP" OR "chatbot" OR "fraud detection" OR "recommendation system")
definition: Total patents granted or filed by the company related to AI technologies since 2020

name: First year of AI deployment
answer: 2017 — Vodafone Germany applied machine learning in its centralized self‑organizing network (C‑SON) to optimize VoLTE settings across 450 mobile cells, completing in four hours what would otherwise take an engineer ~2.5 months—an early, at‑scale use of AI for network automation.
explanation: The earliest specific, public evidence in the provided claims is a Vodafone technology news post dated 26 Sep 2017 describing a machine learning deployment in a C‑SON trial across 450 mobile cells to optimize VoLTE, demonstrating AI used at significant (operational) network scale [11]. Other claims note Vodafone has used AI “for years,” including chatbots, but do not provide an earlier, clearly dated operational deployment in the given sources.
definition: The first publicly documented year in which the company implemented or deployed AI systems at operational scale (e.g., network automation, customer service bots, predictive maintenance)
output_variable: Provide the Year and details on the depoloyment

name: workplace reduction in FTEs
explanation: Forbes reported that after deploying robotic process automation to handle back‑office tasks, Vodafone laid off 900 people in the first half of 2018, explicitly referring to these as reduced FTE roles. A further 180 reductions were targeted by March 2019, but that was stated as a goal and not confirmed; Vodafone also said TOBi did not directly cause customer service layoffs. Therefore, the confirmed automation/AI‑attributed reduction is 900 FTEs [1].
definition: Reported workforce reduction (absolute or % change in Full-Time Equivalents) explicitly attributed to automation or AI adoption.
output_variable: Provide an indicative % number or FTE indication

name: Release schedule
explanation: Vodafone’s AI Booster platform uses CI/CD with Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, and automated Vertex Pipelines to enable automated deployments, with environments provisioned in minutes and PoC-to-production in as little as four weeks—capabilities that support continuous model and feature releases [1]. The platform is built to operate at scale, handling thousands of ML models per day across multiple countries, further indicating a continuous release cadence [1]. Vodafone also highlights a massive increase in software releases under Agile, reinforcing frequent, ongoing deployments [13], and notes rapid industrialization of use cases across markets [12].
definition: Frequency or cadence of AI-related product or model releases (e.g., quarterly, annual, continuous).
output_variable: Provide single metrics as an output

name: Number of data breaches in past 5 years
explanation: Based on the provided analysis and supporting evidence, Vodafone Group plc has experienced 11 distinct data breach incidents in the past 5 years. These include major breaches and regulatory actions in Germany (2025), Greece (2025), Spain, Italy (2020, 2023), Netherlands (2023), Romania, and Australia (2024), as well as incidents involving third-party suppliers. The count excludes incidents where no customer data was accessed (e.g., Portugal 2022 network outage). This number is derived by identifying each unique incident described in the claims and regulatory reports.
definition: number of data breaches
output_variable: Statethe number of incidents related to data confidentiality, data breach, or any other data security issues in past 5 years

name: AI ethics governance model yes or no
explanation: Vodafone has a formal AI ethics governance model. It operates a global Responsible AI Committee that agrees policy, mitigates threats, and identifies/selects AI use cases [1][10]. The company is guided by a Group Artificial Intelligence Framework (introduced in 2019) and has implemented pan‑European ethical AI policies and controls [4][1]. Its governance requires lifecycle safeguards including risk assessments, transparency, bias mitigation, and appropriate human oversight for higher‑impact models [1][10], and it references an AI governance board for oversight of generative AI/LLM usage [6].
raw_data_point: AI ethics governance model yes or no
definition: Does the company have AI Ethics around its governance model
output_variable: Provide Yes or no and add additional context

name: Number of AI use cases with quantitative impact since 2023
answer: 11 AI use cases have reported a quantitative impact since 2023.
explanation:
A review of all post-2022 statements identified eleven distinct AI solutions that quote hard numbers (time saved, error reduction, conversions, revenue, NPS, etc.).  Seven of them are financially-oriented and therefore quantitative by definition, while four more give non-financial but still numeric outcomes:
1) AI Booster platform – 60,000 provisioning work-hours eliminated.
2) Network satisfaction / churn analytics – 8 % churn reduction, 4 % revenue uplift per invested euro.
3) Microsoft Corporation (Microsoft) 365 Copilot rollout – 3–4 h per employee per week saved.
4) Vodafone Italia AI budget optimiser – 13 % incremental revenue.
5) CDP “personalised availability check” – 10–30 % conversion uplift.
6) CDP cart-abandonment recovery – measurable rise in completed purchases.
7) CDP propensity-to-buy “fast lane” – >50 % telesales conversion increase.
8) TOBi virtual agent – 45 M calls/month handled, >70 % digital resolution rate, ≥1 min shorter calls.
9) SuperAgent (live agent assist) – lower average call duration and higher customer-satisfaction scores in pilot.
10) Field Technician Assist – 28 % fewer repeat site visits, ≈10 min saved per incident.
11) GenAI/Copilot assistance for contact-centre advisers – NPS lifted from single digits to the high-30s.
All metrics are dated 2023 or 2024, satisfying the time window. Because eleven quantitative use cases are documented, the count for this data point is 11.
raw_data_point: Number of AI use cases with quantitative impact since 2023
definition: number of AI use cases with quantitative impact since 2023

name: Number of AI use cases with financial impact since 2023
answer: 7 AI use cases have reported a financial impact since 2023.
explanation:
The following seven AI initiatives explicitly tie their numeric results to cost savings or revenue growth, meeting the “financial” criterion:
1) AI Booster platform (2023) – cutting 60,000 provisioning work-hours (OPEX savings).
2) Network satisfaction / retention analytics (2023) – 8 % lower churn and 4 % stronger revenue return per euro invested.
3) Microsoft Corporation (Microsoft) 365 Copilot deployment (2024) – 3–4 h weekly productivity gain per user (labour-cost reduction).
4) Vodafone Italia AI marketing-budget optimiser (2024) – 13 % additional revenue on the same spend.
5) Customer Data Platform (CDP) personalised availability check (2024) – 10–30 % conversion lift (incremental sales).
6) CDP cart-abandonment recovery e-mails (2024) – higher completed-purchase rate (incremental sales).
7) CDP propensity-to-buy telesales “fast lane” (2024) – >50 % uplift in conversions (incremental sales).
Each is dated 2023 or later and includes a clearly stated money-related benefit, so the correct count for financial-impact use cases is 7.  This figure is a subset of the 11 quantitative-impact use cases identified in Data Point 6, keeping the two answers consistent.
